One day I'll tell my story but for now please be careful when dealing with him.

We had Attorney Cross as a GAL. I won’t be leaving the details of everything he did. There’d too many to list.

My experience. If you are a bigger man, athlete, or didn’t go to college, Rob won’t give you a chance to be a father. Before he met me in person, he told me he would do certain things, and then after he met me he changed. He also bullied me in every email (no matter what it was about). His mind was made up the minute he saw me and it was obvious to me.

In the 14 months we had Rob, there were 2 times I accidentally emailed him from my wife’s account. (We’re married so we have access to each others things on our phones). Both times he questioned my Attorney asking if my wife was the one emailing him. Meanwhile, the opposing party’s mom sent emails or was CCed in them, and he didn’t question her. (Even though I had an OP on her for my son about 5 years ago.)

Rob believed things about me without proof, and no matter what I showed him he refused to take it into consideration. The opposing side never had proof based off of their accusations.

Even days later when I was negotiating paying child support on an online portal with my child’s mothers attorney, and he had already given his recommendation, he was chiming in saying he didn’t see why I needed to pay that way. This is just one of the smaller things that he did consistently to draw a wedge between my sons mother and myself.

When I called his office to make a complaint, they told him and he emailed My Attorney fishing for details. Inappropriate.

Ultimately, Rob claimed that when my sons grandma took him in to his office, my 9 year-old son told him that he wanted to be with me every other weekend. I don’t know if that’s true or not but what I do know is Rob told me that my wife would be allowed to bring my son in, to see if he would give a different answer than what he gave when his grandma brought him in. Rob never allowed that to happen. I know that if someone from my family would’ve brought my son in to see Rob my son would’ve said he wants 50-50, because that’s what he says to me almost every time he sees me. He brings this up constantly. My child knows that the other side of his family doesn’t like me so of course if his grandma is bringing him in, he’s not going to say anything different than what they want him to say. (And At our settlement meeting in March, my sons mother admitted that she had been telling our child she wanted him to be with me every other weekend. She admitted she was coaching him.) As a GAL, Rob should’ve taken this into consideration, but instead, he brushed under the rug. (Just like all of the issues my son told him about when I brought him in to see Rob)

Over the last two years, I rearranged my life entirely to have my son 50-50. The opposing party would say I wasn’t stable because I rented instead of owning, so I bought a house less than 1 mile from her. She would say I didn’t have a good enough job (because I was the heavy weight MMA fighter for team USA so I trained constantly) so I quit that and I got on with the union. (Which has free health insurance for my son and has better coverage than hers, but Rob still recommended her insurance). Overall I did get more time with my son because I have more frequent visits due to the midweek visit that’s been implemented, but I should have been given more.

I don’t regret buying a house or quitting fighting. I ended up with a great career and home, and I know I did everything I could for my son. That gives me peace. My son missing out on proper time with me is Rob’s cross to bear. I am not mad or bitter. I just don’t want what happened to my son to happen to anyone else. We had Rob for 14 months and he met with my son 3 times. Twice in March 2022 and once in September 2022. He gave his recommendation off of the September meeting when the grandma brought my son in. He built no rapport in only 3 meetings and didn’t try to see if my son would give a different answer by having someone from my family bring him in.

If you’re looking for a fair attorney, look elsewhere.

It is hard to believe that one week ago today I was sitting in a federal courtroom with members of my family waiting for Judge Lawless to pass sentencing on my oldest nephew for two counts of indictment under federal law. We were shocked and devastated to learn of these charges since they were so out of character for him; however, a year ago he pled guilty to drawing unemployment benefits from the Railroad while employed elsewhere. When questioned why he would make such an unwise decision to do so, he simply stated he was trying to raise two children on his own and provide for them with very little income. He was living in a small town and to get to work, shop for groceries and other amenities it was necessary to travel and the price of gas and events of covid were a contributing factor.

Dan was referred to us by another attorney friend of mine and we were very impressed with his no nonsense demeanor and his non sugar coating explanation of just how serious the charges were against my nephew and he never once promised he could convince the court to be anything but  fair and even convinced us that incarceration was a real probability.

These actions negatively impacted his recent marriage involving two teenage stepdaughters as well as his desire to show his biological children that he was raised to always be honest and ask for help when needed. To say that my nephew was anything but remorseful and angry at himself for causing so many people who loved him such worry and grief during his bonding out period is an understatement. He realized he had finally gained employment through a company he truly loves and threw himself into becoming a valued and humble member of the community by getting involved in amateur radio activities, accepting leadership roles during emergency situations and working very long extra hours to be able to make amends, pay restitution and regain some self worth through his privilege of being allowed to bond out and work throughout the past year while waiting for his sentencing hearing.

We highly respect Mr. Noll for his professional and caring attitude to us during a very trying and frightening phase of our lives and are extremely grateful to Judge Lawless for her leniency and fairness in sentencing. The outcome was very much a surprise in many ways and such a relief and blessing! We would without a doubt  highly recommend Noll Law Office to anyone who is seeking professional guidance and a basic no nonsense approach to dealing with matters way beyond our ability.

Driving home from St Louis, halfway home my path crossed a Sangamon officer in a little sting among the interstate. I was ticketed and Officer Friendly (not his real name) provided anxiety-provoking information about an upcoming mandatory court date and all of the costly fines and court fees I could expect as my punishment.

After losing one night of sleep, I reached out  to family and friends for advice on how to proceed which included finding a Sangamon County attorney. Mr. Hanken came with the highest of recommendations.

I was immediately reassured as Carrie explained what to expect. My presence in court on the “mandatory” date 3 weeks later would NOT be necessary. Scott would be handling everything — and I didn’t need to be in court unless Scott told me I needed to be there.

For a fraction of the price Officer Friendly quoted me, Scott worked on my behalf to review my case, attend my hearings and have the charges successfully dismissed.

Mr. Jones was represented by Mr. Appleton and another attorney in Chicago in 2013-2014. Mr. Jones and his siblings were fraudulently deprived of $300,000 in proceeds from a wrongful death lawsuit from a family member. Mr. Appleton and his co-counsel quickly initiated litigation suing the family member, a legal financing company, and the attorneys who settled the underlying case. Mr. Jones and his family made a full recovery of their damages due to Mr. Appleton and his counsel freezing the family member's bank account, proving to the attorneys and legal financing company that the family member had committed identity theft that could have been stopped, and recovering funds in excess of the actual damages awardable by law in only about one year.

We value client criticism, but it is tough to rationalize how Mr. Jones' recovery could have been better.
